questions.question: What is the topic for Week 29?
questions.explanation: The topic explicitly listed for Lesson Twenty-Nine is COPING WITH LOSS.

questions.question: Which of the following was the topic for Week 28?
questions.explanation: Love Yourself was the topic for Week 28.

questions.question: And they mourned and wept and fasted until evening for Saul and for ________ his son...
questions.explanation: The memory verse (2 Samuel 1:12) says 'for Saul and for Jonathan his son...'

questions.question: Provide the book, chapter, and verse for this week's memory verse.
questions.explanation: The memory verse reference is 2 Samuel 1:12.

questions.question: Type out the memory verse for Week 29 in full (NKJV).
questions.answer: And they mourned and wept and fasted until evening for Saul and for Jonathan his son, for the people of the LORD and for the house of Israel, because they had fallen by the sword.
questions.explanation: Full text recall of 2 Samuel 1:12.

questions.question: According to the memory verse (2 Samuel 1:12), what did the people do until evening for Saul and Jonathan?
options: B. They mourned, wept, and fasted
questions.explanation: The verse says 'And they mourned and wept and fasted until evening...'

questions.question: The introduction states that loss is an inevitable part of life that takes many forms.
questions.explanation: The text begins: 'Loss is an inevitable part of life (Ecclesiastes 3:1-2, 4) that takes many forms...'

questions.question: According to the introduction, what determines our ability to heal and move forward after a loss?
options: A. How quickly we forget about it
B. How we cope with it
C. How much money we have
D. The size of our family
questions.explanation: The text states 'but how we cope with it determines our ability to heal and move forward.'

questions.question: What is the heading of the first outline?
questions.explanation: Outline I is titled 'COPING MECHANISMS'.

questions.question: What is the first step in coping with loss, according to Outline I?
options: A. To hide the pain from others
D. To travel to a new country
questions.explanation: The text states 'The first step in coping with loss is to acknowledge the pain (Psalm 73:26).'

questions.question: According to the lesson, engaging in simple daily tasks can provide stability during an emotionally challenging time.
questions.explanation: The text states 'However, engaging in simple daily tasks can provide stability during an emotionally challenging time (Ruth 2:2).'

questions.question: Which of these is NOT mentioned as a healthy way to express grief in the lesson?
questions.explanation: The text lists 'journaling, tourism, art, music, or physical exercise'. Ignoring the problem is not mentioned.

questions.question: Which scripture is cited to support that the weight of loss is too heavy to bear alone, and seeking professional help is good?
questions.explanation: The text cites '(Proverbs 24:6; 11:14)' after the point on seeking professional help.

questions.question: What is the heading of the second outline?
questions.explanation: Outline II is titled 'FINDING COMFORT IN GOD'.

questions.question: What does Outline II say is a powerful tool when grieving because it provides a direct line of communication with God?
questions.explanation: The text states 'Prayer is a powerful tool when grieving. It provides a direct line of communication with God...'

questions.question: Which scripture is quoted as 'The LORD is near to those who have a broken heart, And saves such as have a contrite spirit'?
questions.explanation: The text says 'Psalm 34:18 says, “The LORD is near to those who have a broken heart...'

questions.question: According to the lesson, trusting that God has a plan, even in times of suffering, can provide a sense of purpose and hope.
questions.explanation: The text states 'trusting that God has a plan, even in times of suffering, can provide a sense of purpose and hope for the future (Romans 8:28; Jeremiah 29:11).'

questions.question: How can engaging with a faith community help during a loss?
options: A. It can provide a sense of belonging and immense comfort
B. It guarantees the loss will be reversed
C. It distracts you completely so you forget
D. It replaces the lost loved one
questions.explanation: The text says 'can provide a sense of belonging, and be an immense source of comfort and support (Galatians 6:2, Hebrews 10:25a).'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited regarding reflecting on the eternal hope offered through Christ, knowing death is not the end?
questions.explanation: The text cites '(1 Thessalonians 4:13-14, Psalm 116:15)' for this point.

questions.question: According to the conclusion, coping with loss is a personal journey, but we can find strength by embracing practical mechanisms and leaning on faith.
questions.explanation: The conclusion explicitly states this.

questions.question: Where is this verse found? 'Then Job arose, tore his robe, and shaved his head; and he fell to the ground and worshiped.'
questions.verse_text: Then Job arose, tore his robe, and shaved his head; and he fell to the ground and worshiped.
questions.explanation: This is verse 20 from the main Bible Passage.

questions.question: According to the Bible Passage (Job 1:21), what did Job say after tearing his robe?
options: B. 'Naked I came from my mother’s womb, And naked shall I return there.'
D. 'I will seek revenge on my enemies.'
questions.explanation: Verse 21 says 'Naked I came from my mother’s womb, And naked shall I return there...'

questions.question: According to Job 1:21, what did Job say about the Lord?
options: B. The Lord has forgotten me
C. The Lord gave, and the Lord has taken away; Blessed be the name of the Lord.
D. The Lord will restore double tomorrow
questions.explanation: Verse 21 says 'The Lord gave, and the Lord has taken away; Blessed be the name of the Lord.'

questions.question: According to Job 1:22, in all this, what did Job NOT do?
options: C. He did not sin nor charge God with wrong
D. He did not speak to anyone
questions.explanation: Verse 22 says 'In all this Job did not sin nor charge God with wrong.'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited to support that surrounding ourselves with a supportive community can provide immense comfort?
questions.explanation: The text says 'knowing that others are there to listen can provide immense comfort (Job 2:11-13; 42:11).'

questions.question: The Bible is filled with verses that speak to loss and grief, offering comfort and ________.
questions.explanation: Outline II says 'The Bible is filled with verses that speak to loss and grief, offering comfort and hope.'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited in Outline II to support trusting that God has a plan?
questions.explanation: Outline II cites '(Romans 8:28; Jeremiah 29:11)' regarding trusting God has a plan.

questions.question: Finding healthy ways like journaling or art to express grief can be therapeutic because they allow us to process our emotions.
questions.explanation: The text says 'can be incredibly therapeutic because they allow us to process our emotions and alleviate the intensity of our grief.'

questions.question: What is essential for healing, according to the first step in Outline I?
options: B. Allowing ourselves to feel the grief
C. Moving to a new house
questions.explanation: The text says 'Allowing ourselves to feel the grief is essential for healing...'

questions.question: Which scriptures are cited in the introduction to show that loss takes many forms like the death of a loved one or broken relationships?
questions.explanation: The text says '...takes many forms like the death of a loved one, broken relationships, major life changes, etc. (Job 14:1-2; 1 Samuel 30:1-4).'
